---
title: "Project Checkpoint 0"
editor: source
---

Before the course starts, you are tasked with ranking the datasets available
for analysis. We have done our best to locate high quality datasets from 
a variety of contexts. This year we have three possible options:

-   [Environmental: Plastic Waste](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2021/2021-01-26/readme.md)
-   [Health: Measles Cases](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2025/2025-06-24/readme.md)
-   [Geopolitical: Government Types](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2024/2024-11-05/readme.md)

Please complete the following Google Form indicating your ranked preference 
for these options. The survey will also ask you if there are individuals with 
whom you would or would not like to work, and what were your *most* and 
*least* favorite topics from Introduction to R. 

[Link to Google Form for Data Preferences](https://forms.gle/W5tbc3AEMxh89d3n8)

<!-- 1.  Rank dataset preferences -->
<!-- 2.  State if there are people that you would trade your dataset preference to work with -->
<!-- 3.  If you had to choose one **favorite** topic from 331 what would it be? -->

<!-- -   viz -->
<!-- -   cleaning data -->
<!-- -   writing functions -->
<!-- -   iterating over functions -->
<!-- -   statistical analyses -->

<!-- 4.  If you had to choose one **least favorite** topic from 331 what would it be? -->

<!-- -   viz -->
<!-- -   cleaning data -->
<!-- -   writing functions -->
<!-- -   iterating over functions -->
<!-- -   statistical analyses -->
